title: Pneumonanthe puberula
taxonomy: Pneumonanthe puberula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Pneumonanthe, which is a monotypic genus meaning it contains only one species. Pneumonanthe puberula is a type of bog bean or marsh gentian, typically found in wetland environments. The plant is native to Europe and western Asia. It is a relatively rare species and is often found in acidic bogs and fens.

intro: Pneumonanthe puberula, also known as Marsh Cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, commonly found in wetlands and marshy areas. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Pneumonanthe puberula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
